title: phyllanthus androgynus
intro: Phyllanthus androgynus, also known as Achiote or Daun Mariani, is a plant species in the family Phyllanthaceae, native to Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Phyllanthus androgynus include the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve liver function. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are often used in teas, infusions, and capsules, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or ointment for skin conditions.
